= Chapter 2: Database Architecture & Backend

== 2.1 Architecture intent and migration posture

Kosha uses a single, idempotent Supabase SQL script as the backend contract source of truth: link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql[supabase/schema.sql].
The script is intentionally written for repeated execution with patterns like:

. Create if not exists for tables and indexes.
. Drop policy if exists before create policy.
. Guarded foreign key creation through pg_constraint checks.
. Guarded realtime publication membership through pg_publication_tables checks.

Primary anchors:
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L1[supabase/schema.sql]
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L114[supabase/schema.sql]
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L218[supabase/schema.sql]
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L86[supabase/schema.sql]

This means onboarding a new environment and re-running schema on existing environments follow the same path with low drift risk.

'''

== 2.2 Core relational schema (required entities)

== 2.2.1 Profiles

Definition:
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L10[supabase/schema.sql]

Purpose:
- One row per authenticated user for app-level identity and onboarding state.

Key columns:
. id as primary key and FK target to auth users.
. display_name.
. monthly_income.
. onboarded.
. created_at.
. avatar_url.

FK contract:
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L117[supabase/schema.sql]

Bootstrap trigger:
- New auth users auto-seed a profiles row using handle_new_user trigger function.
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L302[supabase/schema.sql]
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L323[supabase/schema.sql]

RLS:
- Enabled at link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L212[supabase/schema.sql]
- Own-row select/insert/update policies at link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L219[supabase/schema.sql] and link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L336[supabase/schema.sql]

Operational impact:
- Frontend auth gate and onboarding depend on profile existence and onboarded state.
- link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useAuth.js#L20[src/hooks/useAuth.js]
- link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx#L157[src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx]

'''

== 2.2.2 Transactions

Definition:
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L20[supabase/schema.sql]

Purpose:
- Canonical ledger for income, expense, and investment entries.
- Also stores generated recurring instances and linked entries created by RPCs (bill payments, loan payments).

Key financial columns:
. date.
. type with constrained enum-like check.
. amount with positive constraint.
. category.
. investment_vehicle.
. is_repayment.
. payment_mode with constrained values.

Recurring engine columns:
. is_recurring.
. recurrence.
. next_run_date.
. source_transaction_id.
. is_auto_generated.

Ownership:
- user_id FK added and enforced idempotently.
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L67[supabase/schema.sql]
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L131[supabase/schema.sql]

Performance indexes:
- General and composite user/date/type/category indexes.
- Trigram index for description search.
- Partial indexes for recurring and source linkage.
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L180[supabase/schema.sql]

Realtime:
- Included in supabase_realtime publication.
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L80[supabase/schema.sql]

RLS:
- Enabled at link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L213[supabase/schema.sql]
- Own-row CRUD policies at link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L235[supabase/schema.sql]
- Consolidated all-operation policy also exists at link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L348[supabase/schema.sql]

Frontend usage:
- Core hook and query key engine:
  - link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useTransactions.js#L177[src/hooks/useTransactions.js]
- Recurring materialization RPC call:
  - link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useTransactions.js#L88[src/hooks/useTransactions.js]

'''

== 2.2.4 Budgets (important dual-structure note)

There are two budget-related tables in schema, but only one is actively wired in frontend.

Legacy-style budgets table:
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L1037[supabase/schema.sql]
- RLS enabled at link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L1049[supabase/schema.sql]
- No explicit policies are declared immediately after enabling RLS.

Active category budgets table:
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L1096[supabase/schema.sql]
- Per-user per-category monthly_limit.
- Unique index user_id + category.
- Own-row CRUD policies at link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L1126[supabase/schema.sql]

Actual frontend integration:
- The app reads and writes category_budgets, not public.budgets:
  - link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useBudgets.js#L18[src/hooks/useBudgets.js]
  - link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useBudgets.js#L59[src/hooks/useBudgets.js]

Architectural implication:
. public.budgets currently behaves like a reserved or transitional table.
. category_budgets is the real production budget data model.

'''

. created_at.

FK constraints:

  - link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/invites.js#L61[src/lib/invites.js]

Security nuance:
- Select policy uses true for authenticated users to allow token-based validation.
- Consumption safety is still enforced by update policy checks and used_by null semantics.
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L277[supabase/schema.sql]
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L287[supabase/schema.sql]

Storage integration:
- Private bug-reports bucket with MIME and size controls.
- Per-user folder ownership policies on storage.objects.

== 2.3 RLS implementation deep dive (scalar subquery model)

Kosha consistently uses this form across policies:

(select auth.uid()) = owner_column

Representative anchors:
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L221[supabase/schema.sql]
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L237[supabase/schema.sql]
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L349[supabase/schema.sql]

Why scalar subqueries are used:
. Predictable evaluation shape inside policy expressions.
. Consistent style across USING and WITH CHECK clauses.
. Better planner behavior in many Supabase/Postgres RLS workloads versus repeatedly embedding function calls in more complex boolean expressions.
. Clear auditability: every policy visibly ties to auth.uid ownership semantics.

USING vs WITH CHECK in this schema:
. USING controls which existing rows are visible or targetable for read/update/delete.
. WITH CHECK controls which new row values are allowed on insert/update.

Examples:
- Insert ownership check:
  - link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L241[supabase/schema.sql]
- Update ownership guard:
  - link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L266[supabase/schema.sql]

Invite consume policy demonstrates mixed-mode authorization:
. Creator can update invite rows they created.
. Non-creator can consume only if row is unclaimed and they set used_by to themselves with used_at present.
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L287[supabase/schema.sql]

Storage RLS uses folder-based user partitioning:
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L453[supabase/schema.sql]

'''

== 2.4 Custom RPC architecture and rationale

The four core RPCs are explicitly documented in schema as performance and safety remediations:
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L611[supabase/schema.sql]

== 2.4.1 get_running_balance

Definition:
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L628[supabase/schema.sql]

Contract:
. Input: p_user_id, p_end_date.
. Output: one numeric scalar.
. Semantics: sum income as positive and non-income as negative through end date.

Why this design:
. Replaces client-side full-row scans and JavaScript summation.
. Returns one number instead of potentially large datasets.
. Leverages database aggregation directly, reducing network and browser CPU.

Security:
- security invoker, still constrained by table RLS and caller context.
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L635[supabase/schema.sql]

Frontend consumers:
- link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useTransactions.js#L605[src/hooks/useTransactions.js]
- link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L776[src/App.jsx]

'''

== 2.4.2 get_month_summary

Definition:
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L812[supabase/schema.sql]

Contract:
. Input: p_user_id, p_year, p_month.
. Output rows grouped by type, is_repayment, category, investment_vehicle with summed total.

Why this design:
. Pushes group-by work into Postgres.
. Returns small aggregate rowsets instead of raw transactions.
. Provides a stable summary contract that frontend parser can normalize.

Frontend parser and call sites:
- Parser: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useTransactions.js#L15[src/hooks/useTransactions.js]
- Hook call: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useTransactions.js#L457[src/hooks/useTransactions.js]
- Prefetch calls: link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L174[src/App.jsx] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L762[src/App.jsx]

'''

== 2.4.3 get_year_summary

Definition:
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L855[supabase/schema.sql]

Contract:
. Input: p_user_id, p_year.
. Output single row containing:
   1. monthly_data JSON array.
   2. category_data JSON object.
   3. vehicle_data JSON object.
   4. totals JSON object.
   5. top5_expenses JSON array.

Implementation characteristics:
. Materializes year slice once into temp table _yr.
. Derives all aggregates from _yr to avoid repeated base table scans.
. Returns pre-shaped analytics payloads aligned to charting needs.

Why marked volatile:
- Uses temp table creation inside plpgsql body.
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L868[supabase/schema.sql]
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L879[supabase/schema.sql]

Frontend normalization and call sites:
- Hook call and normalization:
  - link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useTransactions.js#L480[src/hooks/useTransactions.js]
- App prefetch:
  - link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L209[src/App.jsx]
- Card-level usage:
  - link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/cards/analytics/YoYCards.jsx#L21[src/components/cards/analytics/YoYCards.jsx]

'''

== 2.4.4 mark_liability_paid

Definition:
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L954[supabase/schema.sql]

Contract:
. Input: liability id and user id.
. Output: JSON containing transaction_id, liability_id, and optionally next_due_date.

Atomic transaction flow inside function:
. Lock liability row with for update.
. Validate existence and unpaid status.
. Insert linked expense transaction.
. Mark liability as paid and attach linked transaction id.
. If recurring, create the next liability row.

Why this design:
. Fixes split-brain risk from multi-call client workflows.
. Guarantees all-or-nothing consistency between liability and transaction records.
. Encapsulates recurring roll-forward logic at database boundary.

Frontend usage:
- Direct RPC invocation:
  - link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useLiabilities.js#L157[src/hooks/useLiabilities.js]

'''

== 2.5 Bug report backend RPC and function pipeline

submit_bug_report RPC:
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L472[supabase/schema.sql]

Behavior:
. Auth required through auth.uid guard.
. Input validation for title, description, severity normalization.
. Per-user rate limit: maximum 5 reports in 2 minutes.
. Duplicate detection by fingerprint + route in 7-day window.
. Duplicate path increments occurrence_count and merges metadata/tags.
. New path inserts normalized bug record and returns report id.

Edge notification:
- Function reads bug row with service role client.
- Generates signed screenshot URL if present.
- Posts to Discord or Slack-compatible webhook payload.
- Updates notified_at after successful webhook dispatch.
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/functions/bug-report-notify/index.ts#L33[supabase/functions/bug-report-notify/index.ts]
- link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/functions/bug-report-notify/index.ts#L87[supabase/functions/bug-report-notify/index.ts]

'''

== 2.6 Backend integrity checklist for incoming engineers

. When adding a table:
- Mirror the idempotent DDL style used in link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql#L1[supabase/schema.sql].
- Add FK guards and indexes.
- Enable RLS and define explicit policies for each required operation.

. When adding an RPC:
- Prefer security invoker unless there is a strict reason for definer.
- Ensure all ownership constraints are validated in SQL body.
- Return payloads shaped for direct frontend consumption to reduce client-side reshaping.

. When changing ownership logic:
- Keep scalar auth.uid subquery pattern consistent for policy readability and predictability.
- Update both narrow and convenience policy blocks where overlapping policies exist.

. Current notable schema state:
- public.budgets exists with RLS enabled but no active frontend path.
- category_budgets is the active production budget store.
- Confirm intended deprecation or convergence path before future budgeting feature work.
